   124→
   125→== Overview
   126→
   127→The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.
   128→
   129→BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.
   130→
   131→For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.
   132→
   133→== Tabtarget Vesture
   134→
   135→[[bust_tabtarget]]
   136→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   137→{bust_tabtarget}::
   138→Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
   139→Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
   140→+
   141→* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
   142→* separator: `.`
   143→* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
   144→* envelope: `.sh`
   145→+
   146→Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   147→+
   148→Examples:
   149→+
   150→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.sh` — RBW workbench, ConnectBottle operation
   151→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
   152→* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
   153→* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation
   154→
   155→== Dispatch
   156→
   157→The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
   158→A
   159→{busdl_launcher}
   160→validates arguments and delegates to a
   161→{busdm_formulary}.
   162→The
   163→{busdc_colophon}
   164→identifies the routing path;
   165→the
   166→{busdf_frontispiece}
   167→provides human-readable description;
   168→the
   169→{busdi_imprint}
   170→embeds target parameters.
   171→
   172→[[busdc_colophon]]
   173→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   174→{busdc_colophon}::
   175→The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
   176→A
   177→{busdc_colophon}
   178→determines which
   179→{busdm_formulary}
   180→handles the dispatch.
   181→Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
   182→+
   183→The
   184→{busdc_colophon}
   185→is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
   186→+
   187→Examples:
   188→+
   189→* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
   190→* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
   191→* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench
   192→
   193→[[busdf_frontispiece]]
   194→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   195→{busdf_frontispiece}::
   196→The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
   197→A
   198→{busdf_frontispiece}
   199→uses PascalCase and describes the operation.

200→+
   201→Appears after the
   202→{busdc_colophon}
   203→and dot separator.
   204→+
   205→Examples: `Build`, `ConnectBottle`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.
   206→
   207→[[busdi_imprint]]
   208→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   209→{busdi_imprint}::
   210→An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
   211→An
   212→{busdi_imprint}
   213→specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
   214→+
   215→Appears after a second dot separator following the
   216→{busdf_frontispiece}.
   217→+
   218→Examples:
   219→+
   220→* `nsproto` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   221→* `srjcl` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.srjcl.sh`
   222→
   223→[[busdm_formulary]]
   224→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   225→{busdm_formulary}::
   226→A component that routes
   227→{busdc_colophon_s}
   228→to implementations.
   229→Each
   230→{busdm_formulary}
   231→registers handlers for a set of
   232→{busdc_colophon_s}
   233→and dispatches to the appropriate function.
   234→+
   235→Types:
   236→+
   237→* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
   238→* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
   239→+
   240→A
   241→{busdm_formulary}
   242→is sourced by the
   243→{busdl_launcher}
   244→and provides the dispatch function.
   245→
   246→[[busdl_launcher]]
   247→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   248→{busdl_launcher}::
   249→A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
   250→{busdm_formulary}.
   251→A
   252→{busdl_launcher}
   253→is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
   254→+
   255→Naming follows
   256→{bust_tabtarget}:
   257→`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   258→+
   259→Responsibilities:
   260→+
   261→* Parse
   262→{busdc_colophon}
   263→from own filename
   264→* Validate required arguments
   265→* Source the target
   266→{busdm_formulary}
   267→* Invoke the dispatched function
   268→
   269→== Routing Behavior
   270→
   271→=== Dispatch Flow
   272→
   273→. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   274→. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
   275→* {busdc_colophon}:
   276→`rbw-B`
   277→* {busdf_frontispiece}:
   278→`ConnectBottle`
   279→* {busdi_imprint}:
   280→`nsproto`
   281→. Launcher sources the appropriate
   282→{busdm_formulary}
   283→(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
   284→. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
   285→. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
   286→
   287→=== Workbench Pattern
   288→
   289→[source,bash]
   290→----
   291→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
   292→
   293→{prefix}w_dispatch() {
   294→    local frontispiece="$1"
   295→    local imprint="$2"
   296→    shift 2
   297→
   298→    case "$frontispiece" in
   299→        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;

300→        ConnectBottle)  {prefix}w_connect_bottle "$imprint" "$@" ;;
   301→        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
   302→    esac
   303→}
   304→----
   305→
   306→=== Testbench Pattern
   307→
   308→Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
   309→
   310→[source,bash]
   311→----
   312→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
   313→
   314→{prefix}t_dispatch() {
   315→    local frontispiece="$1"
   316→    shift
   317→
   318→    case "$frontispiece" in
   319→        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
   320→        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
   321→        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
   322→    esac
   323→}
   324→----
   325→
   326→=== Colophon Conventions
   327→
   328→{busdc_colophon_s}
   329→follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
   330→
   331→* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
   332→* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
   333→* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-B-` for bottle operations)
   334→
   335→The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
   336→
   337→== Regime Configuration
   338→
   339→BUK defines two
   340→{axrg_regime_s}
   341→for project and developer configuration.
   342→{axrg_assignment_s}
   343→use
   344→{axf_bash}
   345→format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).
   346→
   347→=== Regime Prefixes
   348→
   349→[[burc_prefix]]
   350→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   351→{burc_prefix}::
   352→Bash Utility Configuration Regime
   353→
   354→[[burs_prefix]]
   355→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   356→{burs_prefix}::
   357→Bash Utility Station Regime
   358→
   359→[[bure_prefix]]
   360→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   361→{bure_prefix}::
   362→Bash Utility Environment Regime
   363→
   364→=== Kit Regime Operations
   365→
   366→[[bukro_kindle]]
   367→// ⟦axvr_kindle⟧
   368→{bukro_kindle}::
   369→Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
   370→{axrg_assignment_s}.
   371→Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.
   372→
   373→[[bukro_validate]]
   374→// ⟦axvr_validate⟧
   375→{bukro_validate}::
   376→Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
   377→{axrg_regime_s}.
   378→Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.
   379→
   380→[[bukro_render]]
   381→// ⟦axvr_render⟧
   382→{bukro_render}::
   383→Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
   384→{axrg_regime}
   385→state.
   386→
   387→=== BURC Regime
   388→
   389→[[burc_regime]]
   390→// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
   391→{burc_regime}::
   392→Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
   393→BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.
   394→
   395→[[burc_station_file]]
   396→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   397→{burc_station_file}::
   398→Path to the developer's personal
   399→{burs_regime}

400→assignment file.
   401→
   402→[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
   403→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   404→{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
   405→Directory containing
   406→{busdl_launcher}
   407→scripts.
   408→
   409→[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
   410→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string⟧
   411→{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
   412→Single-character token separator in
   413→{bust_tabtarget}
   414→inscriptions.
   415→
   416→[[burc_tools_dir]]
   417→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   418→{burc_tools_dir}::
   419→Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.
   420→
   421→[[burc_project_root]]
   422→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   423→{burc_project_root}::
   424→Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.
   425→
   426→[[burc_managed_kits]]
   427→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string⟧
   428→{burc_managed_kits}::
   429→Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
   430→At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
   431→At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.
   432→
   433→[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
   434→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   435→{burc_temp_root_dir}::
   436→Root directory for BUK temporary files.
   437→
   438→[[burc_output_root_dir]]
   439→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   440→{burc_output_root_dir}::
   441→Root directory for BUK command output files.
   442→
   443→[[burc_log_last]]
   444→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname⟧
   445→{burc_log_last}::
   446→Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.
   447→
   448→[[burc_log_ext]]
   449→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname⟧
   450→{burc_log_ext}::
   451→File extension for log files, without leading dot.
   452→
   453→=== BURS Regime
   454→
   455→[[burs_regime]]
   456→// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
   457→{burs_regime}::
   458→Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
   459→BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.
   460→
   461→[[burs_log_dir]]
   462→// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
   463→{burs_log_dir}::
   464→Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.
   465→
   466→=== BURD Regime
   467→
   468→[[burd_prefix]]
   469→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   470→{burd_prefix}::
   471→Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime
   472→
   473→[[burd_regime]]
   474→// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton⟧
   475→{burd_regime}::
   476→Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
   477→Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
   478→The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.
   479→
   480→==== Input Variables
   481→
   482→[[burd_group_input]]
   483→// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
   484→{burd_group_input}::
   485→Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
   486→
   487→[[burd_verbose]]
   488→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer⟧
   489→{burd_verbose}::
   490→Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
   491→0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.
   492→
   493→[[burd_regime_file]]
   494→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   495→{burd_regime_file}::
   496→Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
   497→
   498→[[burd_no_log]]
   499→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧

500→{burd_no_log}::
   501→When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
   502→
   503→[[burd_interactive]]
   504→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧
   505→{burd_interactive}::
   506→When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.
   507→
   508→[[burd_color]]
   509→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   510→{burd_color}::
   511→Color output policy.
   512→Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
   513→Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.
   514→
   515→[[burd_coordinator_script]]
   516→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧

   519→
   520→[[burd_launcher]]
   521→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧
   522→{burd_launcher}::
   523→Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
   524→

   526→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧

   529→Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

   537→==== Computed Variables
   538→
   539→[[burd_group_computed]]
   540→// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
   541→{burd_group_computed}::
   542→Variables derived during dispatch setup.
   543→
   544→[[burd_now_stamp]]
   545→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   546→{burd_now_stamp}::
   547→Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
   548→
   549→[[burd_temp_dir]]
   550→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   551→{burd_temp_dir}::
   552→Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.
   553→
   554→[[burd_output_dir]]
   555→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   556→{burd_output_dir}::
   557→Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.
   558→
   559→[[burd_transcript]]
   560→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   561→{burd_transcript}::
   562→Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.
   563→
   564→[[burd_git_context]]
   565→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   566→{burd_git_context}::
   567→Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.
   568→
   569→[[burd_log_last]]
   570→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   571→{burd_log_last}::
   572→Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.
   573→
   574→[[burd_log_same]]
   575→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   576→{burd_log_same}::
   577→Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.
   578→
   579→[[burd_log_hist]]
   580→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
   581→{burd_log_hist}::
   582→Path to the timestamped historical log file.
   583→
   584→==== Parsed Variables
   585→
   586→[[burd_group_parsed]]
   587→// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
   588→{burd_group_parsed}::
   589→Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.
   590→
   591→[[burd_command]]
   592→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   593→{burd_command}::
   594→Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).
   595→
   596→[[burd_target]]
   597→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   598→{burd_target}::
   599→Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

600→
   601→[[burd_cli_args]]
   602→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated⟧
   603→{burd_cli_args}::
   604→Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.
   605→
   606→[[burd_token_1]]
   607→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   608→{burd_token_1}::
   609→Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).
   610→
   611→[[burd_token_2]]
   612→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   613→{burd_token_2}::
   614→Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).
   615→
   616→[[burd_token_3]]
   617→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   618→{burd_token_3}::
   619→Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).
   620→
   621→[[burd_token_4]]
   622→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   623→{burd_token_4}::
   624→Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.
   625→
   626→[[burd_token_5]]
   627→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
   628→{burd_token_5}::
   629→Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.
   630→
   631→=== BURE Regime
   632→
   633→[[bure_regime]]
   634→// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton⟧
   635→{bure_regime}::
   636→Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
   637→Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
   638→Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.
   639→
   640→[[bure_countdown]]
   641→// ⟦axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧
   642→{bure_countdown}::
   643→Countdown behavior override.
   644→When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
   645→When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
   646→Any other value fails validation.

   124→
   125→== Overview
   126→
   127→The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.
   128→
   129→BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.
   130→
   131→For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.
   132→
   133→== Tabtarget Vesture
   134→
   135→[[bust_tabtarget]]
   136→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   137→{bust_tabtarget}::
   138→Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
   139→Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
   140→+
   141→* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
   142→* separator: `.`
   143→* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
   144→* envelope: `.sh`
   145→+
   146→Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   147→+
   148→Examples:
   149→+
   150→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.sh` — RBW workbench, ConnectBottle operation
   151→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
   152→* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
   153→* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation
   154→
   155→== Dispatch
   156→
   157→The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
   158→A
   159→{busdl_launcher}
   160→validates arguments and delegates to a
   161→{busdm_formulary}.
   162→The
   163→{busdc_colophon}
   164→identifies the routing path;
   165→the
   166→{busdf_frontispiece}
   167→provides human-readable description;
   168→the
   169→{busdi_imprint}
   170→embeds target parameters.
   171→
   172→[[busdc_colophon]]
   173→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   174→{busdc_colophon}::
   175→The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
   176→A
   177→{busdc_colophon}
   178→determines which
   179→{busdm_formulary}
   180→handles the dispatch.
   181→Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
   182→+
   183→The
   184→{busdc_colophon}
   185→is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
   186→+
   187→Examples:
   188→+
   189→* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
   190→* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
   191→* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench
   192→
   193→[[busdf_frontispiece]]
   194→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   195→{busdf_frontispiece}::
   196→The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
   197→A
   198→{busdf_frontispiece}
   199→uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
   200→+
   201→Appears after the
   202→{busdc_colophon}
   203→and dot separator.
   204→+
   205→Examples: `Build`, `ConnectBottle`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.
   206→
   207→[[busdi_imprint]]
   208→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   209→{busdi_imprint}::
   210→An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
   211→An
   212→{busdi_imprint}
   213→specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
   214→+
   215→Appears after a second dot separator following the
   216→{busdf_frontispiece}.
   217→+
   218→Examples:
   219→+
   220→* `nsproto` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   221→* `srjcl` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.srjcl.sh`
   222→
   223→[[busdm_formulary]]
   224→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   225→{busdm_formulary}::
   226→A component that routes
   227→{busdc_colophon_s}
   228→to implementations.
   229→Each
   230→{busdm_formulary}
   231→registers handlers for a set of
   232→{busdc_colophon_s}
   233→and dispatches to the appropriate function.
   234→+
   235→Types:
   236→+
   237→* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
   238→* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
   239→+
   240→A
   241→{busdm_formulary}
   242→is sourced by the
   243→{busdl_launcher}
   244→and provides the dispatch function.
   245→
   246→[[busdl_launcher]]
   247→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   248→{busdl_launcher}::
   249→A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
   250→{busdm_formulary}.
   251→A
   252→{busdl_launcher}
   253→is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
   254→+
   255→Naming follows
   256→{bust_tabtarget}:
   257→`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   258→+
   259→Responsibilities:
   260→+
   261→* Parse
   262→{busdc_colophon}
   263→from own filename
   264→* Validate required arguments
   265→* Source the target
   266→{busdm_formulary}
   267→* Invoke the dispatched function
   268→
   269→== Routing Behavior
   270→
   271→=== Dispatch Flow
   272→
   273→. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   274→. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
   275→* {busdc_colophon}:
   276→`rbw-B`
   277→* {busdf_frontispiece}:
   278→`ConnectBottle`
   279→* {busdi_imprint}:
   280→`nsproto`
   281→. Launcher sources the appropriate
   282→{busdm_formulary}
   283→(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
   284→. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
   285→. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
   286→
   287→=== Workbench Pattern
   288→
   289→[source,bash]
   290→----
   291→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
   292→
   293→{prefix}w_dispatch() {
   294→    local frontispiece="$1"
   295→    local imprint="$2"
   296→    shift 2
   297→
   298→    case "$frontispiece" in
   299→        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
   300→        ConnectBottle)  {prefix}w_connect_bottle "$imprint" "$@" ;;
   301→        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
   302→    esac
   303→}
   304→----
   305→
   306→=== Testbench Pattern
   307→
   308→Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
   309→
   310→[source,bash]
   311→----
   312→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
   313→
   314→{prefix}t_dispatch() {
   315→    local frontispiece="$1"
   316→    shift
   317→
   318→    case "$frontispiece" in
   319→        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
   320→        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
   321→        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
   322→    esac
   323→}
   324→----
   325→
   326→=== Colophon Conventions
   327→
   328→{busdc_colophon_s}
   329→follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
   330→
   331→* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
   332→* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
   333→* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-B-` for bottle operations)
   334→
   335→The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
   336→
   337→== Regime Configuration
   338→
   339→BUK defines two
   340→{axrg_regime_s}
   341→for project and developer configuration.
   342→{axrg_assignment_s}
   343→use
   344→{axf_bash}
   345→format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).
   346→
   347→=== Regime Prefixes
   348→
   349→[[burc_prefix]]
   350→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   351→{burc_prefix}::
   352→Bash Utility Configuration Regime
   353→
   354→[[burs_prefix]]
   355→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   356→{burs_prefix}::
   357→Bash Utility Station Regime
   358→
   359→[[bure_prefix]]
   360→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
   361→{bure_prefix}::
   362→Bash Utility Environment Regime
   363→
   364→=== Kit Regime Operations
   365→
   366→[[bukro_kindle]]
   367→// ⟦axvr_kindle⟧
   368→{bukro_kindle}::
   369→Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
   370→{axrg_assignment_s}.
   371→Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.
   372→
   373→[[bukro_validate]]
   374→// ⟦axvr_validate⟧
   375→{bukro_validate}::

== Overview

The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.

BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.

For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.

== Tabtarget Vesture

[[bust_tabtarget]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
{bust_tabtarget}::
Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
+
* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
* separator: `.`
* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
* envelope: `.sh`
+
Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.sh` — RBW workbench, ConnectBottle operation
* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation

== Dispatch

The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
A
{busdl_launcher}
validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
The
{busdc_colophon}
identifies the routing path;
the
{busdf_frontispiece}
provides human-readable description;
the
{busdi_imprint}
embeds target parameters.

[[busdc_colophon]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{busdc_colophon}::
The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdc_colophon}
determines which
{busdm_formulary}
handles the dispatch.
Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
+
The
{busdc_colophon}
is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench

[[busdf_frontispiece]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{busdf_frontispiece}::
The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdf_frontispiece}
uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
+
Appears after the
{busdc_colophon}
and dot separator.
+
Examples: `Build`, `ConnectBottle`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.

[[busdi_imprint]]
// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
{busdi_imprint}::
An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
An
{busdi_imprint}
specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
+
Appears after a second dot separator following the
{busdf_frontispiece}.
+
Examples:
+
* `nsproto` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
* `srjcl` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.srjcl.sh`

[[busdm_formulary]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
{busdm_formulary}::
A component that routes
{busdc_colophon_s}
to implementations.
Each
{busdm_formulary}
registers handlers for a set of
{busdc_colophon_s}
and dispatches to the appropriate function.
+
Types:
+
* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
+
A
{busdm_formulary}
is sourced by the
{busdl_launcher}
and provides the dispatch function.

[[busdl_launcher]]
// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
{busdl_launcher}::
A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
A
{busdl_launcher}
is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
+
Naming follows
{bust_tabtarget}:
`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Responsibilities:
+
* Parse
{busdc_colophon}
from own filename
* Validate required arguments
* Source the target
{busdm_formulary}
* Invoke the dispatched function

== Routing Behavior

=== Dispatch Flow

. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
* {busdc_colophon}:
`rbw-B`
* {busdf_frontispiece}:
`ConnectBottle`
* {busdi_imprint}:
`nsproto`
. Launcher sources the appropriate
{busdm_formulary}
(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
. Target function executes with imprint as parameter

=== Workbench Pattern

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh

{prefix}w_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    local imprint="$2"
    shift 2

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        ConnectBottle)  {prefix}w_connect_bottle "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Testbench Pattern

Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh

{prefix}t_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    shift

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Colophon Conventions

{busdc_colophon_s}
follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:

* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-B-` for bottle operations)

The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.

== Regime Configuration

BUK defines two
{axrg_regime_s}
for project and developer configuration.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use
{axf_bash}
format (shell variable assignment files that are sourced).

=== Regime Prefixes

[[burc_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{burc_prefix}::
Bash Utility Configuration Regime

[[burs_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{burs_prefix}::
Bash Utility Station Regime

[[bure_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{bure_prefix}::
Bash Utility Environment Regime

=== Kit Regime Operations

[[bukro_kindle]]
// ⟦axvr_kindle⟧
{bukro_kindle}::
Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
{axrg_assignment_s}.
Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.

[[bukro_validate]]
// ⟦axvr_validate⟧
{bukro_validate}::
Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime_s}.
Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.

[[bukro_render]]
// ⟦axvr_render⟧
{bukro_render}::
Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime}
state.

=== BURC Regime

[[burc_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
{burc_regime}::
Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.

[[burc_station_file]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_station_file}::
Path to the developer's personal
{burs_regime}
assignment file.

[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
Directory containing
{busdl_launcher}
scripts.

[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string⟧
{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
Single-character token separator in
{bust_tabtarget}
inscriptions.

[[burc_tools_dir]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_tools_dir}::
Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.

[[burc_project_root]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_project_root}::
Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.

[[burc_managed_kits]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string⟧
{burc_managed_kits}::
Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.

[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_temp_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK temporary files.

[[burc_output_root_dir]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burc_output_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK command output files.

[[burc_log_last]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname⟧
{burc_log_last}::
Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.

[[burc_log_ext]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname⟧
{burc_log_ext}::
File extension for log files, without leading dot.

=== BURS Regime

[[burs_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton⟧
{burs_regime}::
Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.

[[burs_log_dir]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path⟧
{burs_log_dir}::
Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton⟧
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required⟧
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer⟧
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_git_context}::
Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.

[[burd_log_last]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_log_last}::
Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.

[[burd_log_same]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_log_same}::
Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.

[[burd_log_hist]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path⟧
{burd_log_hist}::
Path to the timestamped historical log file.

==== Parsed Variables

[[burd_group_parsed]]
// ⟦axl_voices axhrgb_group⟧
{burd_group_parsed}::
Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.

[[burd_command]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_command}::
Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).

[[burd_target]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_target}::
Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

[[burd_cli_args]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated⟧
{burd_cli_args}::
Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.

[[burd_token_1]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_token_1}::
Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).

[[burd_token_2]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_token_2}::
Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).

[[burd_token_3]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_token_3}::
Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).

[[burd_token_4]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_token_4}::
Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.

[[burd_token_5]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{burd_token_5}::
Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.

=== BURE Regime

[[bure_regime]]
// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton⟧
{bure_regime}::
Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.

[[bure_countdown]]
// ⟦axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional⟧
{bure_countdown}::
Countdown behavior override.
When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
Any other value fails validation.

[[bure_verbose]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer⟧
{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
// ⟦axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string⟧
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.
